change: Add a new return value to StateTransition.TransitionDb() (signature becomes (*ExecutionResult, <new>, error)).

impact.why: Sole non-test direct caller: returns NewStateTransition(...).TransitionDb(); must propagate/adapt the new return value. This is the wrapper through which nearly all callers reach TransitionDb.
Calls ApplyMessage (l.115) and consumes result; receives the propagated new value from TransitionDb.
Drives applyTransaction per tx; 2-hop transitive caller of TransitionDb via ApplyMessage.
Exported wrapper calling applyTransaction->ApplyMessage; a primary propagation point for the extra return value.
Calls ApplyMessage; would need to handle the new return (currently discards result).
core.ApplyMessage at l.227; gas estimation consumes ExecutionResult and must accept the new value.
core.ApplyMessage at l.1196 in the eth_call path; consumes result, must adapt to new return.
core.ApplyMessage at l.1702 (access-list creation path); propagates the extra return value.
core.ApplyMessage at l.257 when replaying txs to reach a target index.
Multiple ApplyMessage calls (l.549, 708, 820, 1004) across trace/replay paths; each must thread the new return value.
core.ApplyMessage at l.240 (msgResult); the t8n tool reads ExecutionResult and would consume the new value.
core.ApplyTransaction at l.977 -> applyTransaction -> ApplyMessage; 2-hop caller producing block receipts.
ApplyTransaction at l.121; block generation path reaches TransitionDb via ApplyMessage.

grep_would_miss: core.ApplyMessage (the wrapper) — a grep for 'TransitionDb' only hits state_transition.go:210 + tests; every real consumer reaches it through ApplyMessage and would be invisible to a TransitionDb name-grep.
core.ApplyTransaction / applyTransaction / Process — 2-hop wrappers reached via ApplyMessage, not named TransitionDb.
miner.worker.commitTransaction and core.BlockGen.AddTxWithChain — reached via ApplyTransaction, two hops removed from the seed name.
notes: TransitionDb is called once in non-test code (ApplyMessage); the real blast radius is the ApplyMessage/ApplyTransaction wrapper tree, which cks find_callers(TransitionDb, depth=2) surfaced well (it returned the ApplyMessage caller fan-out). _test.go callers (tracers tests, state_test_util) were excluded per instructions as they don't encode an external contract. eth/tracers/api.go entries collapsed into one row spanning the four ApplyMessage sites to avoid double-counting the same file/behavior.
